messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0170",
  "title": "ToBid",
  "page_index": 97,
  "page_range": "97"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0171",
    "title": "效率重构周期：2025走向理性，2026走向结构红利",
    "page_range": "97",
    "summary": "**Topics & Entities:** Mobile ad monetization industry, developers, advertisers, application/user/ad placement value ecosystems.\n**Dates & Phases:** 2025 (transition to rationality and “efficiency-first” model); 2026 (focus on “structural dividends” and mining existing traffic value).\n**Definitions & Concepts:** Shift from “scale-first” to “efficiency-first”; evolution from “single-point eCPM optimization” to “overall revenue optimization”; structural value release.\n**Metrics:** eCPM, fill rate, frequency capping, long-term ARPDAU.\n**Procedures & Strategies:** Replacing simple price sorting with global revenue judgment; integrating fill rate, frequency capping, user experience, and long-term ARPDAU into a unified decision framework; combining systematic platform capabilities with deep vertical-specific business understanding.\n**Claims:** Slowing traffic growth and tighter advertiser budgets make short-term eCPM chasing unsustainable. Future industry growth will derive from optimizing existing traffic rather than volume expansion. Generic monetization solutions are losing effectiveness.\n**Evidence Types:** Industry trend analysis, strategic forecasting, conceptual framework comparison. (No tables or figures referenced in this section.)\n**Answerable Retrieval Questions:** What are the projected strategic shifts in mobile ad monetization for 2025 and 2026? How should developers transition from eCPM-focused tactics to holistic revenue optimization? Which metrics must be integrated into modern ad decision frameworks? Why are generic monetization tools becoming less effective, and what capabilities are now required for sustainable commercial growth?"
  }
]

message.content: This section details the strategic evolution of the mobile ad monetization industry, targeting developers, advertisers, and application/user/ad placement value ecosystems. It outlines a two-phase efficiency reconstruction cycle: 2025 focuses on a transition to rationality and an “efficiency-first” model, while 2026 targets “structural dividends” through the optimization of existing traffic. Key conceptual shifts include moving from “scale-first” to “efficiency-first” paradigms and evolving from single-point eCPM optimization to holistic overall revenue optimization. Core metrics integrated into modern decision frameworks include eCPM, fill rate, frequency capping, and long-term ARPDAU. Recommended procedures involve replacing basic price sorting with global revenue judgment, unifying performance and user experience metrics into a single decision framework, and combining systematic platform capabilities with deep vertical-specific business insights. The section claims that slowing traffic growth and tighter advertiser budgets make short-term eCPM chasing unsustainable, asserting that future growth will stem from optimizing existing traffic rather than volume expansion, while generic monetization solutions are losing effectiveness. These points are supported by industry trend analysis, strategic forecasting, and conceptual framework comparisons; no tables or figures are referenced in this section.

Recommended procedures involve replacing simple price sorting with global revenue judgment, unifying multiple performance and experience metrics into a single decision framework, and combining systematic platform capabilities with deep vertical-specific business insights. The section claims that slowing traffic growth and constrained advertiser budgets render short-term eCPM chasing unsustainable, positioning future industry growth on optimizing existing traffic rather than volume expansion, while noting the declining effectiveness of generic monetization tools. response_text: This section details the strategic evolution of the mobile ad monetization industry, targeting developers, advertisers, and application/user/ad placement value ecosystems. It outlines a two-phase efficiency reconstruction cycle: 2025 focuses on a transition to rationality and an “efficiency-first” model, while 2026 targets “structural dividends” through the optimization of existing traffic. Key conceptual shifts include moving from “scale-first” to “efficiency-first” paradigms and evolving from single-point eCPM optimization to holistic overall revenue optimization. Core metrics integrated into modern decision frameworks include eCPM, fill rate, frequency capping, and long-term ARPDAU. Recommended procedures involve replacing basic price sorting with global revenue judgment, unifying performance and user experience metrics into a single decision framework, and combining systematic platform capabilities with deep vertical-specific business insights. The section claims that slowing traffic growth and tighter advertiser budgets make short-term eCPM chasing unsustainable, asserting that future growth will stem from optimizing existing traffic rather than volume expansion, while generic monetization solutions are losing effectiveness. These points are supported by industry trend analysis, strategic forecasting, and conceptual framework comparisons; no tables or figures are referenced in this section.
